title: Stelis cylindrica
intro: Stelis cylindrica, also known as the cylindrical Stelis, is a species of orchid found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stelis cylindrica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis cylindrica include teas and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Stelis cylindrica belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is due to its shared characteristics and similarities with other orchid species. Within the Orchidaceae family, Stelis cylindrica is further classified as a member of the tribe Epidendreae. The tribe Epidendreae is a group of orchids that share a common evolutionary history and distinct morphological features. Further classification of Stelis cylindrica into a specific genus and species within the tribe Epidendreae is still being researched.
uses: Stelis cylindrica has been reported to have potential medicinal uses, with its extracts showing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
